description: An authenticated/per-user response cached at the shared edge because it lacked private/no-store, leaking data across users.
body: Failure mode: a response containing user-specific data is served without Cache-Control: private/no-store, so the CDN caches it and serves one user's content to others. Detection signals: cache-status HIT on an authenticated endpoint; users report seeing each other's data; missing private directive on dynamic responses. Do not let per-user responses reach the shared cache. Safer fallback: set Cache-Control: private, no-store on authenticated responses, scope public caching to truly static content, and purge the leaked objects immediately.
